summary: Re-tested SEO meta on /jobs and /internships. /jobs fix verified PASS. /internships fix FAILS — title still renders 'Live Jobs & Internships · SkillEn' instead of 'Internship Openings · SkillEn'.
passed: /jobs document.title contains 'Live Jobs & Internships' ('Live Jobs & Internships · SkillEn')

failed.issue: document.title on /internships is 'Live Jobs & Internships · SkillEn' — does NOT contain 'Internship Openings'. og:title also reflects the Jobs page title, not the Internships title.
failed.evidence: Root cause: Jobs.jsx derives the page via `const { type: routeType } = useParams(); const isInternships = routeType === 'internships';` — but App.js registers static routes `<Route path='/jobs' />` and `<Route path='/internships' />` (no `:type` param). Therefore `useParams()` returns no `type`, `isInternships` is always false on /internships, and <Seo title={isInternships ? 'Internship Openings' : 'Live Jobs & Internships'} /> always renders the Jobs title. Observed at https://skill-launch-3.preview.emergentagent.com/internships → title 'Live Jobs & Internships · SkillEn'.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/Jobs.jsx, detect the route by pathname instead of useParams. Replace `const { type: routeType } = useParams(); const isInternships = routeType === 'internships';` with `import { useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; const location = useLocation(); const isInternships = location.pathname.startsWith('/internships');` — OR change App.js routes to `<Route path='/:type(jobs|internships)' element={...} />` so useParams populates `type`.
